description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

On April 14 (Tuesday, Reiwa 6), Saitama Prefecture's first-ever emergency bear culling drill was held in Hanno City. At the field training exercise at Hanno Riverbed, Saitama Prefecture, Saitama Prefectural Police, Hanno Police Station, Hanno Hunting Association, and our city worked together to confirm emergency culling procedures and other important steps.

Bear hibernation has ended, and the chance of encountering bears is rising. Emergency cullings are now happening across the country. We cannot predict when such a situation might become necessary here.

Hanno City's emergency culling manual was only recently created. Through this drill, I believe we identified areas that will need review.

Moving forward, we will continue to work closely with all related parties to ensure the safety and peace of mind of our residents and visitors to Hanno City.

Note: No bears have actually been sighted at Hanno Riverbed, the site of this drill. The area will be busy this season as a spot for barbecue and river activities. Hanno City is full of beautiful natural scenery and tourist attractions beyond just the riverbed. Please do visit.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/shiseijoho/shichoshitsu/shichoblog/shisei2026/shisei202604/13458.html
summary: 📝 Mayor's Reflection: Saitama Prefecture Emergency Bear Culling Drill
